title: Erigeron elatus
intro: Erigeron elatus, also known as Fleabane, is a flowering plant species belonging to the daisy family (Asteraceae) and is native to the southwestern United States and northern Mexico. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, offering health benefits such as anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antiseptic effects, which can aid in wound healing and alleviate various digestive issues. The bioactive constituents of Erigeron elatus include flavonoids, sesquiterpene lactones, and phenolic acids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. In traditional herbal medicine, Erigeron elatus is often used to make teas, tinctures, and infusions, which are commonly prepared to treat various health conditions, including skin irritations and gastrointestinal issues.

The species Erigeron elatus is native to the southwestern region of the United States and northern Mexico. The plant is often referred to as a tall fleabane or a tall daisy due to its characteristics and appearance.
